summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a high‑energy Rochester show highlighted by a soaring Let It Grow and an unforgettable second set.
review: The SBD source is clear and well‑balanced, letting the band’s mid‑80s fire shine through. Reviewers rave about the first‑set country‑western medley and a second‑set that many rank among their top ten, with Let It Grow, High Time and Deal standing out. The crowd energy – banners, gooney birds and dual drum kits – is captured vividly, making this a must‑listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten enthusiastic reviews (mostly 4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the clear soundboard recording; few minor complaints keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear SBD with solid balance; occasional minor quirks mentioned but overall very listenable and faithful to the live vibe.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A country‑western flavored first set (China Cat Sunflower > Loser > Uncle John’s Band > Mexicali Blues > Cumberland Blues) builds into a high‑energy second set that many fans rank among the best of the era, punctuated by banner drops and memorable crowd moments.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir was spotted sitting onstage playing slide, adding a fun visual element.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland gets multiple shout‑outs (“Brent rocks!”) for his organ work and stage presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann grabs the mic to lead crowd clapping; reviewers note the dual‑drum‑kit setup adding extra power.
